video_title: Top #DDD Fried Food Videos with Guy Fieri (Part 2) | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Twisted Soul
restaurants.guy_intro: If you're in Atlanta, Georgia, you've got to check out Twisted Soul. At Twisted Soul here, they have something for everyone. Southern with international flair that took off thanks to Debra Van Treese, a former flight attendant who came up with her own flight plan.

dishes.description: Chicken marinated for 24 hours in seasoning salt, garlic powder, onion powder, and black pepper, then dredged in flour and house seasoning, and cast iron fried.
dishes.guy_response: That's just good, old-fashioned, honest fried chicken.

dishes.description: Collard greens prepared in a unique roll format.
dishes.guy_response: I want to order a hundred of these. Congratulations for wrecking collard greens for the rest of my life. Still to date, one of my favorite things I ever had on Triple D.

restaurants.name: Soul Avenue Kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: We're in Moorhead, Minnesota, to check out a joint where the chef is doing farm-to-table, Midwest-style like you've never seen. This is Soul Avenue Kitchen. Soul Avenue is global street food with a Midwest interpretation.

dishes.description: Boneless chicken thighs marinated overnight in egg, buttermilk, flour, pepper, salt, then double-dredged in seasoned flour and double-fried. Served on a toasted bun with lime aioli, kimchi, som jang, and house pickles.
dishes.guy_response: My friend, that is excellent fried chicken. That is super crunch and tastiest. I have a chicken joint called Chicken Guy, and I say that sauce is the boss. When I watch you put on all that som jang, I'm like, just call it the som jang sandwich. But it's this balance that goes on between the fat of that lime aioli with the acid of the pickle. It's like clashing right there in the middle of the chicken sandwich. Ba-boom. Dude, outstanding.

restaurants.name: Nexus Brewery and Restaurant
restaurants.guy_intro: So here I am about 10 minutes north of downtown Albuquerque, New Mexico, right off the I-25. We're here to see one in particular, this joint. Nexus Brewery and Restaurant.

dishes.description: Chicken brined for 24 hours in water, seasoned salt, sugar, chopped garlic, and onions. Then dipped in an egg wash with Frank's hot sauce and water, dredged in flour (with salt and pepper), and fried for about 10 minutes. Served with collard greens and mashed potatoes with gravy.
dishes.guy_response: Man, there's a ton of crunch. But it's not, the brine's not too heavy. And even though you had all that hot sauce in there, it's not spicy. It's just really flavorful. Nice mashed potatoes. Good. Crunchy chicken. A lot of flavor. And my favorite out of all of them is the greens, which I can't believe I'm finding collard greens in New Mexico. That's mandatory right there.

restaurants.name: Ramona Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm in Ramona, California, right at the gateway of the desert. In the middle of town is this little joint, Ramona Cafe, owned by Sonya van der Veen, the daughter of a local egg farmer. It's a small town landmark, a 26-year-old local legend, serving homemade chicken cordon bleu with fresh hollandaise, deep fried.

dishes.description: Chicken breast pounded thin, filled with ham and Gruyere Swiss cheese, rolled up, double-dipped in straight egg, and double-breaded in homemade breading (from dried biscuits, flour, pepper, cayenne, poultry seasoning, salt), then deep-fried.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. That cheese really stands out. Doesn't it? Yeah. It really stands out in that. Definitely is the crunchiest. I mean, that is a texture on that.

dishes.description: A 50-50 mixture of ground beef and veal, breaded with homemade breading (from dried biscuits, flour, pepper, cayenne, poultry seasoning, salt), and fried.
dishes.guy_response: Wow, I think the veal and the beef idea of chicken fried steak is nice, big, very flavorful, fluffy. It's got a little bit of a peppery taste to it. Okay. That is really good. The breading's not too thick. And the meat is nice and moist inside. Yes, yeah. That's what I like about it. There's a lot of flavor. You can actually taste meat. You're not just tasting fried batter, fried thing.

restaurants.name: West Side Drive-In
restaurants.guy_intro: It was back in 2009 when I first met Chef Lou Aaron at his old-school 50-style diner in Boise, Idaho. These days, he's almost got the trifecta, a diner and a drive-in. All that's missing is the dive. This is West Side Drive-In.

dishes.description: Triple-decker sandwich with Texas toast, chicken, Swiss cheese, cheddar cheese, ham, garlic butter, dipped in egg, breaded with panko breadcrumbs, and deep-fried. Served with raspberry coulis.
dishes.guy_response: Money up. The crispiness comes out in the way that they batter it. When you bite into it, it just explodes in your mouth. It's really juicy, and that flavor comes through. Then you dip it in some jam. It's incredible.

restaurants.name: Joe's Gizzard City
restaurants.guy_intro: Well, unless you're in Potterville, Michigan, where they ask, hey, how do you want your gizzards? Welcome to Joe's Gizzard City. Purely one of a kind.

dishes.description: Chicken gizzards pressure-cooked with garlic powder, celery salt, and salt to tenderize, then battered in a secret special flour mix and deep-fried.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. They're tender. You know what? I don't think. I think they're quite tasty, actually. I think the seasoning that you pressure cook them in, that makes a big difference. The batter is killer. Don't get gizzards anywhere else. These gizzards are the best.

restaurants.name: Curry Fried Chicken
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Salt Lake City, Utah. When you see a fried chicken place, you look closer at the sign and say, wait a second. Does that say curry in front of it? I got to check it out. This is curry fried chicken.

dishes.description: Chicken brined for 24 hours in water, vinegar, lemon juice, black pepper, salt, curry powder, house garam masala, ginger, and garlic. Then dredged in house spice and fried for about 15 minutes. Served with lentil curry, mixed veggie curry, rice, ranch chili sambal, and pita.
dishes.guy_response: If all you sold was spicy lentils, I would show up. That's it. The lentils are really gangster. The fried chicken. That's good crisp on that. I like to get the pita bread. Put everything in it and just make a little bite of it. It's good stuff. Mmm. To get all that curry spice, then you get the moisture and the fat of the chicken. Wow, that's a lot of flavor. Rob. Oh, real deal. This chicken is unique. It wrecks fried chicken. There's so much flavor in it. It wrecks it. It's very crunchy, very juicy.

restaurants.name: T-Bone Tom's
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm south of Houston, almost by Galveston, in a little town called Kemah. There's this great little eatery here, T-Bone Tom's, where these folks have been hand cutting their steaks for over 35 years.

dishes.description: Brisket rubbed with T-Bone's father's recipe dry rub (salt, granulated onion, granulated garlic, black pepper, paprika, red pepper), then smoked for about 16 hours between 165 and 175 degrees.
dishes.guy_response: Look at that smoke ring right there. Mm. Now pick up the smoke flavor, but not overbearing. The fat in the meat, now it's just going to melt. Mm. It doesn't get any more tender than that.

dishes.description: Pickled jalapenos, seeded and deveined, stuffed with chopped smoked brisket and homemade barbecue sauce. Then rolled in flour, buttermilk, and panko breadcrumbs, and deep-fried at 350 degrees. Served with ranch dressing.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, yeah. Wow. I mean, the barbecue's great. The jalapeno being a pickled jalapeno really makes it. That's an added little flavor that you wouldn't expect. Mmm. They're killer. I mean, killer, killer.

restaurants.name: Sauce Boss Southern Kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here at Draper, Utah, southwest of Salt Lake City. This joint's got character, a story, and real food for real people. This is Sauce Boss Southern Kitchen.

dishes.description: Chicken (drums and thighs) dredged in all-purpose flour seasoned with lemon pepper, garlic salt, seasoning salt, parsley, chili powder, and rosemary. Then dipped in an egg and milk mixture, and back into the seasoned flour. Rested for 6 hours to overnight, then deep-fried at 350 degrees for 8 minutes.
dishes.guy_response: I'm a thigh guy. This is as big as my head. You've built so much flavor all the way through this. Crunchy, juicy chicken.

dishes.description: Bacon rendered down, then onion garlic puree added. Cooked in boiling water with salt, pepper, brown sugar, butter, and red wine vinegar for 2.5-3 hours.
dishes.guy_response: Dime right. Tender. A lot of vinegar, but not too much. A lot of sugar, but not too much. This is a really good green, man. Delicious.

dishes.description: Onion garlic puree sauteed with salt and pepper, added to heavy cream, Parmesan cheese, and cheddar cheese. Pre-cooked pasta added to the hot cheese sauce to heat up and finish.
dishes.guy_response: The pasta should have some texture. I don't want just this cheese and some garlic. I want cheese and some other matter. A cheddar milkshake. Exactly.

restaurants.name: Eischen's Bar
restaurants.guy_intro: When I think small town, middle America, I think farming and families. How about a place that's been open for business for more than 110 years and serves over 24,000 pieces of fried chicken a week? I had to come check out Eischen's Bar here in Okarche, Oklahoma.

dishes.description: Whole cut-up bird breaded in a secret mix of wheat flour, cornmeal, salt, baking powder, sugar, paprika, and other spices. Deep-fried at 330 degrees for 15 minutes.
dishes.guy_response: Excellent, man. Super tender and juicy. You just, the skin turns into a nice crust because of the flour and the cornmeal. But boy, it's good. It's cooled down to a point now where I can just start shoveling it in. The most tender, the juiciest chicken I've ever had. Tastes like my grandmother's chicken. It's crispy. It's crunchy.
